candidate_check.description: The Ubuntu operating system must record time stamps for audit records that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) or Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).

check_content_excerpt:
To verify the time zone is configured to use UTC or GMT, run the following command.  
 
$ timedatectl status | grep -i "time zone" 
Timezone: UTC (UTC, +0000) 
 
If "Timezone" is not set to UTC or GMT, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
To configure the system time zone to use UTC or GMT, run the following command, replacing [ZONE] with UTC or GMT: 
 
$ sudo timedatectl set-timezone [ZONE]
title: The Ubuntu operating system must record time stamps for audit records that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) or Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).
